forensic_findings.finding: P5's executed checks validate only registry-entry schema completeness.
evidence: The runner's validate_registry() requires one entry per RULE-004..RULE-010 and requires fields id, owner, scope, condition, approved_action, prohibited_action, version.
The runner's negative_tests() only removes prohibited_action from an in-memory registry candidate and expects schema validation to reject it.

forensic_findings.finding: P5 provides no execution path that exercises workspace promotion, managed-channel transport, rule-update dedup/version conflict, independent test assignment, director conflict/global policy mutation, or product-feedback application.
P5's own non_claim limits the evidence to registry schema enforcement and mutation rejection, while its acceptance field all_target_rules_enforced=true is broader than that stated scope.
RV2's classification matches the actual P5 test scope: RULE-004 is code-enforced only for registry schema; RULE-005..RULE-010 are policy-only and runtime NOT_ENFORCED.

resolution.p5_runtime_execution_happened: A runner execution may have happened, but the available evidence establishes registry-schema validation only.
resolution.root_cause: P5 conflated successful registry-schema validation for each rule record with operational enforcement of each rule's approved/prohibited actions.

release_effect.p5_may_not_be_used_for: A claim that RULE-005..RULE-010 are operationally runtime-enforced.

non_claims: This receipt does not rewrite either historical P5 or RV2 artifact.
This receipt does not establish a live regression, because no evidence shows that operational guards ever existed and were later removed.
This receipt does not issue a P7 PASS or FAIL verdict.
